terms.definition: An ordered, indexed sequence of elements stored in contiguous memory, addressed by an integer index.
An array that grows and shrinks at runtime by re-allocating a larger backing buffer when capacity runs out; Python's `list` is one.
An integer position into a sequence, starting at 0; supports negative values that count from the end.
A sub-range of a sequence selected with `lst[a:b]`; in Python lists this allocates and copies a new list.
The average cost per operation over a long sequence; Python's `list.append` is amortized O(1) despite occasional O(n) resizes.
The ability to read any element in O(1) by index, a defining property of arrays.
